{{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Yuge Clan | image = | caption = | home_province = | titles = | founding_year = | dissolution_year = | notable_members = | related_clans = {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe clan|qq=}} }} '''Yuge Clan''' (弓削氏, ゆげうじ) was a [[clan]] that adopted "Yuge" as its name.
The Yuge clan led the [[Yuge no Be]] (弓削部), a group responsible for making bows in ancient Japan. The clan had multiple lineages, each with distinct ancestral traditions and regions of influence. The Yuge clan had close ties with the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe clan|qq=}}, with some lineages identifying as its cadet branches. Sub-branches included the {{ill|Hiraiwa Clan|ja|平岩氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiraiwa Clan|qq=}}.
== Lineages ==
The Yuge clan consisted of several lineages, making it difficult to categorize historical figures into specific branches. Based on the 9th-century ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Shōjiroku|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=}}'', the Yuge clan can be broadly classified into three lineages:
* '''Heavenly Lineage (Tenjin系)''' : This lineage claimed descent from {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高魂尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|qq=}} through {{ill|Amewakahiko|ja|天日鷲翔矢命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amewakahiko|qq=}}<ref name="s">''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Shōjiroku|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen Shōjiroku|qq=}}'', Left Capital, Kami Lineage</ref> (also known as {{ill|Amebihashikakeyu no Mikoto|ja|天毘和志可気流夜命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amebihashikakeyu no Mikoto|qq=}}<ref>''Shinsen Shōjiroku'', Kawachi, Kami Lineage</ref>), serving as the principal administrators ({{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|lt=Tomonomiyatsuko|lt=Tomo no Miyatsuko|ja|伴造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomo no Miyatsuko|qq=}}) of the [[Yuge no Be]]. Their [[kabane]] was ''Muraji,'' later changed to ''Asomi'' during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}}.<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|13|12|2}}</ref>
* '''Descendants of the Heavenly Grandchild (Tenson系, Mononobe Lineage)''' : {{main|Yuge clan#Relationship with Mononobe clan}} : A branch of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe clan|qq=}}.<ref>''Shinsen Shōjiroku'', Left Capital, Kami Lineage: "Yuge Sukune, same ancestor as Ishigami."</ref> {{ill|Mononobe no Moriya|ja|物部守屋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe no Moriya|qq=}} reportedly adopted the maternal surname Yuge O-muraji, and his descendants took the Yuge surname. Members of this lineage included {{ill|Dōkyō|ja|道鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōkyō|qq=}} and his brother {{ill|Yuge no Kiyoto|ja|弓削浄人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge no Kiyoto|qq=}}. With Dōkyō's favor under {{ill|Empress Kōken|ja|孝謙天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Kōken|qq=}} (later {{ill|Empress Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Shōtoku|qq=}}), the family gained significant prominence, with Yuge no Kiyoto achieving the Rank of {{ill|Dainagon|ja|大納言|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dainagon|qq=}}, and ten family members attaining Ranks of Fifth or higher. Previously bearing the kabane ''Muraji,'' Kiyoto was renamed ''Yuge Mikiyo Asomi,'' while others were renamed ''Yuge Asomi'' or ''Yuge Sukune.'' Following Dōkyō's downfall, their kabane reverted, and while some retained the title ''Yuge Sukune'' and held official posts, they failed to pass on their Ranks to subsequent generations.
* '''Earthly Deities Lineage (Chigi系)''' : This lineage traced its ancestry to {{ill|Nekitsuma|ja|禰伎都麻|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nekitsuma|qq=}}, who was said to have been born from the water where {{ill|Ameoshihone no Mikoto|ja|天押穂根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameoshihone no Mikoto|qq=}} washed his hands.<ref name="s" />
== Relationship with Mononobe Clan ==
The Yuge clan, based in Kawachi, shared a geographical and occupational connection with the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe clan|qq=}}. Some scholars view them as separate groups,<ref>Yokota, Ken'ichi, ''Dōkyō,'' pp. 41–42.</ref> while others consider them a cadet branch.<ref>Takikawa, Masajirō, "Yuge no Dōkyō," in ''Jinbutsu Shin Nihonshi,'' pp. 193–194.</ref> The early Heian-period ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sendai Kuji Hongi|qq=}}'' states that {{ill|Mononobe no Oikoshi|ja|物部尾輿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe no Oikoshi|qq=}} married Asa-hime and Kawaru-hime, women of the Yuge clan. Their child, Moriya, adopted the title Yuge O-muraji.<ref>''Sendai Kuji Hongi,'' Volume 5, ''Tenson Hongi.''</ref> The ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}}'' refers to Moriya as "Mononobe Yuge Moriya,"<ref>{{nihonshoki|Bidatsu|1|4|}}</ref> indicating his maternal lineage.<ref>Yokota, Ken'ichi, ''Dōkyō,'' p. 36.</ref>
A decree issued by Empress Kōken in {{ill|Tenpyō-hōji 8|ja|天平宝字|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō-hōji 8|qq=}} (764) suggests that Dōkyō sought to succeed his ancestor's position as Minister. The "Minister" likely referred to Moriya, the last Mononobe O-muraji.<ref>Yokota, Ken'ichi, ''Dōkyō,'' p. 35.</ref>
The ''Nihon Shoki'' recounts that after Moriya's defeat, his children scattered, with half of his slaves and property allocated to {{ill|Shitennō-ji|ja|四天王寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitennō-ji|qq=}}.<ref>{{nihonshoki|Suiko|1|1|}}</ref> The ''Shitennōji Ancient Records,'' written around the 10th century, describes descendants settling in five villages named Yuge. This supports the view that Moriya's descendants became part of the Yuge clan.<ref>Yokota, Ken'ichi, ''Dōkyō,'' pp. 39–40.</ref>
== Kibi Yuge Clan == The {{ill|Kibi Yuge no Be|ja|吉備弓削部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Yuge no Be|qq=}} included figures such as {{ill|Kibi Yuge no Kokū|ja|吉備弓削部虚空|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Yuge no Kokū|qq=}}. In {{ill|Bizen Province|ja|備前|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bizen Province|qq=}}, {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|饒速日命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=}}, a supposed ancestor of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe clan|qq=}}, was linked to the enshrinement of the {{ill|Totsuka-no-Tsurugi|ja|十握剣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Totsuka-no-Tsurugi|qq=}} at {{ill|Isonokami Futsunomitama Shrine|ja|石上布都魂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isonokami Futsunomitama Shrine|qq=}}. Nearby, various occupational groups formed, including one in modern {{ill|Kumenan|ja|久米南町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumenan|qq=}}, Okayama Prefecture. ''{{ill|Hōnen|ja|法然|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōnen|qq=}}'''s parents reportedly prayed at {{ill|Honzan-ji|ja|本山寺 (美咲町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honzan-ji|qq=}} in [[Misaki, Okayama]]. The temple was founded in 1110 by ''Yuge Shiko.'' Nearby is {{ill|Renkyū-ji|ja|蓮久寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Renkyū-ji|qq=}}, said to be the clan's ancestral temple.
== Distribution of Kawachi Yuge Clan == In modern {{ill|Yao, Osaka|ja|八尾市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yao, Osaka|qq=}}, the old course of the {{ill|Yamato River|ja|大和川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato River|qq=}} separates former {{ill|Shiki District, Osaka|ja|志紀郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki District, Osaka|qq=}} (Yuge) and {{ill|Wakae District, Osaka|ja|若江郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakae District, Osaka|qq=}} (Higashi Yuge). After the Edo-period river engineering, the stream is now {{ill|Nagase River|ja|長瀬川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagase River|qq=}}, though it was the Yamato River's main course in ancient times. Two {{ill|Yuge shrines|ja|弓削神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge shrines|qq=}} still exist on either side of the river. During the Nara period, {{ill|Yuge-ji|ja|弓削寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge-ji|qq=}}, which Empress Kōken visited, also stood here, but its location is now unknown.
{{ill|Yuge no Kiyoto|ja|弓削浄人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge no Kiyoto|qq=}}, Dōkyō's brother, hailed from {{ill|Wakae District, Osaka|ja|若江郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakae District, Osaka|qq=}}, but the Yuge clan appears to have had a broader distribution within Kawachi. In 764, figures such as Yuge Hirotari and Yuge no Okimaro resided in {{ill|Shibukawa District, Osaka|ja|渋川郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibukawa District, Osaka|qq=}}. Empress Kōken granted tax exemptions to {{ill|Ōgata District, Osaka|ja|大県郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgata District, Osaka|qq=}} and Wakae District, while halving taxes in {{ill|Asuka District, Osaka|ja|安宿郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka District, Osaka|qq=}} and Shiki District.
== Notes == {{reflist|2}}
== References == * {{Citation|太田亮|title=姓氏家系大辞典|publisher=Kadokawa Shoten|year=1963}} * {{Citation|佐伯有清|title=日本古代氏族事典|publisher=Yūzankaku|year=1994|author=中村英重}} * Takikawa, Masajirō, "Yuge no Dōkyō," in ''Jinbutsu Shin Nihonshi,'' Meiji Shoin, 1953 * Hōga, Toshio, ''Kozoku Keifu Shūsei,'' Ancient Clan Research Society, 1986 * Yokota, Ken'ichi, ''Dōkyō,'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, New Edition, 1988
== Historical Records == * ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'' * ''Nihon Shoki'' * ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' * ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku''

{{ill|Yuge no Kiyoto|ja|弓削浄人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge no Kiyoto|qq=}}, Dōkyō's brother, hailed from {{ill|Wakae District, Osaka|ja|若江郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakae District, Osaka|qq=}}, but the Yuge clan appears to have had a broader distribution within Kawachi. In 764, figures such as Yuge Hirotari and Yuge no Okimaro resided in {{ill|Shibukawa District, Osaka|ja|渋川郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibukawa District, Osaka|qq=}}. Empress Kōken granted tax exemptions to {{ill|Ōgata District, Osaka|ja|大県郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgata District, Osaka|qq=}} and Wakae District, while halving taxes in {{ill|Asuka District, Osaka|ja|安宿郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka District, Osaka|qq=}} and Shiki District.
== Notes == {{reflist|2}}
== References == * {{Citation|太田亮|title=姓氏家系大辞典|publisher=Kadokawa Shoten|year=1963}} * {{Citation|佐伯有清|title=日本古代氏族事典|publisher=Yūzankaku|year=1994|author=中村英重}} * Takikawa, Masajirō, "Yuge no Dōkyō," in ''Jinbutsu Shin Nihonshi,'' Meiji Shoin, 1953 * Hōga, Toshio, ''Kozoku Keifu Shūsei,'' Ancient Clan Research Society, 1986 * Yokota, Ken'ichi, ''Dōkyō,'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, New Edition, 1988

[[sn:Yuge clan]] [[en:Yuge clan]] [[ja:弓削氏]] [[sn:uge clan]]
<!--Yuge clan--><!--Yuge clan-->

revision.comment: Bot: replace {{ill}} with enwiki link via Wikidata
revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Yuge Clan | image = | caption = | home_province = | titles = | founding_year = | dissolution_year = | notable_members = | related_clans = [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] }} '''Yuge Clan''' (弓削氏, ゆげうじ) was a [[clan]] that adopted "Yuge" as its name.
The Yuge clan led the [[Yuge no Be]] (弓削部), a group responsible for making bows in ancient Japan. The clan had multiple lineages, each with distinct ancestral traditions and regions of influence. The Yuge clan had close ties with the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]], with some lineages identifying as its cadet branches. Sub-branches included the {{ill|Hiraiwa Clan|ja|平岩氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiraiwa Clan|qq=}}.
== Lineages ==
The Yuge clan consisted of several lineages, making it difficult to categorize historical figures into specific branches. Based on the 9th-century ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'', the Yuge clan can be broadly classified into three lineages:
* '''Heavenly Lineage (Tenjin系)''' : This lineage claimed descent from {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高魂尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|qq=}} through {{ill|Amewakahiko|ja|天日鷲翔矢命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amewakahiko|qq=}}<ref name="s">''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'', Left Capital, Kami Lineage</ref> (also known as {{ill|Amebihashikakeyu no Mikoto|ja|天毘和志可気流夜命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amebihashikakeyu no Mikoto|qq=}}<ref>''Shinsen Shōjiroku'', Kawachi, Kami Lineage</ref>), serving as the principal administrators ({{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|lt=Tomonomiyatsuko|lt=Tomo no Miyatsuko|ja|伴造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomo no Miyatsuko|qq=}}) of the [[Yuge no Be]]. Their [[kabane]] was ''Muraji,'' later changed to ''Asomi'' during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}}.<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|13|12|2}}</ref>
* '''Descendants of the Heavenly Grandchild (Tenson系, Mononobe Lineage)''' : {{main|Yuge clan#Relationship with Mononobe clan}} : A branch of the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]].<ref>''Shinsen Shōjiroku'', Left Capital, Kami Lineage: "Yuge Sukune, same ancestor as Ishigami."</ref> [[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Mononobe no Moriya]] reportedly adopted the maternal surname Yuge O-muraji, and his descendants took the Yuge surname. Members of this lineage included [[:en:Dōkyō|Dōkyō]] and his brother {{ill|Yuge no Kiyoto|ja|弓削浄人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge no Kiyoto|qq=}}. With Dōkyō's favor under [[:en:Empress Kōken|Empress Kōken]] (later {{ill|Empress Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Shōtoku|qq=}}), the family gained significant prominence, with Yuge no Kiyoto achieving the Rank of [[:en:Dainagon|Dainagon]], and ten family members attaining Ranks of Fifth or higher. Previously bearing the kabane ''Muraji,'' Kiyoto was renamed ''Yuge Mikiyo Asomi,'' while others were renamed ''Yuge Asomi'' or ''Yuge Sukune.'' Following Dōkyō's downfall, their kabane reverted, and while some retained the title ''Yuge Sukune'' and held official posts, they failed to pass on their Ranks to subsequent generations.
* '''Earthly Deities Lineage (Chigi系)''' : This lineage traced its ancestry to {{ill|Nekitsuma|ja|禰伎都麻|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nekitsuma|qq=}}, who was said to have been born from the water where {{ill|Ameoshihone no Mikoto|ja|天押穂根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameoshihone no Mikoto|qq=}} washed his hands.<ref name="s" />
== Relationship with Mononobe Clan ==
The Yuge clan, based in Kawachi, shared a geographical and occupational connection with the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]]. Some scholars view them as separate groups,<ref>Yokota, Ken'ichi, ''Dōkyō,'' pp. 41–42.</ref> while others consider them a cadet branch.<ref>Takikawa, Masajirō, "Yuge no Dōkyō," in ''Jinbutsu Shin Nihonshi,'' pp. 193–194.</ref> The early Heian-period ''[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' states that [[:en:Mononobe no Okoshi|Mononobe no Oikoshi]] married Asa-hime and Kawaru-hime, women of the Yuge clan. Their child, Moriya, adopted the title Yuge O-muraji.<ref>''Sendai Kuji Hongi,'' Volume 5, ''Tenson Hongi.''</ref> The ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'' refers to Moriya as "Mononobe Yuge Moriya,"<ref>{{nihonshoki|Bidatsu|1|4|}}</ref> indicating his maternal lineage.<ref>Yokota, Ken'ichi, ''Dōkyō,'' p. 36.</ref>
A decree issued by Empress Kōken in [[:en:Tenpyō-hōji|Tenpyō-hōji]] (764) suggests that Dōkyō sought to succeed his ancestor's position as Minister. The "Minister" likely referred to Moriya, the last Mononobe O-muraji.<ref>Yokota, Ken'ichi, ''Dōkyō,'' p. 35.</ref>
The ''Nihon Shoki'' recounts that after Moriya's defeat, his children scattered, with half of his slaves and property allocated to [[:en:Shitennō-ji|Shitennō-ji]].<ref>{{nihonshoki|Suiko|1|1|}}</ref> The ''Shitennōji Ancient Records,'' written around the 10th century, describes descendants settling in five villages named Yuge. This supports the view that Moriya's descendants became part of the Yuge clan.<ref>Yokota, Ken'ichi, ''Dōkyō,'' pp. 39–40.</ref>
== Kibi Yuge Clan == The {{ill|Kibi Yuge no Be|ja|吉備弓削部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Yuge no Be|qq=}} included figures such as {{ill|Kibi Yuge no Kokū|ja|吉備弓削部虚空|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Yuge no Kokū|qq=}}. In [[:en:Bizen|Bizen Province]], {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|饒速日命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=}}, a supposed ancestor of the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]], was linked to the enshrinement of the {{ill|Totsuka-no-Tsurugi|ja|十握剣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Totsuka-no-Tsurugi|qq=}} at {{ill|Isonokami Futsunomitama Shrine|ja|石上布都魂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isonokami Futsunomitama Shrine|qq=}}. Nearby, various occupational groups formed, including one in modern [[:en:Kumenan|Kumenan]], Okayama Prefecture. ''[[:en:Hōnen|Hōnen]]'''s parents reportedly prayed at {{ill|Honzan-ji|ja|本山寺 (美咲町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honzan-ji|qq=}} in [[Misaki, Okayama]]. The temple was founded in 1110 by ''Yuge Shiko.'' Nearby is {{ill|Renkyū-ji|ja|蓮久寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Renkyū-ji|qq=}}, said to be the clan's ancestral temple.
== Distribution of Kawachi Yuge Clan == In modern [[:en:Yao, Osaka|Yao, Osaka]], the old course of the [[:en:Yamato River|Yamato River]] separates former {{ill|Shiki District, Osaka|ja|志紀郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki District, Osaka|qq=}} (Yuge) and {{ill|Wakae District, Osaka|ja|若江郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakae District, Osaka|qq=}} (Higashi Yuge). After the Edo-period river engineering, the stream is now {{ill|Nagase River|ja|長瀬川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagase River|qq=}}, though it was the Yamato River's main course in ancient times. Two {{ill|Yuge shrines|ja|弓削神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge shrines|qq=}} still exist on either side of the river. During the Nara period, {{ill|Yuge-ji|ja|弓削寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge-ji|qq=}}, which Empress Kōken visited, also stood here, but its location is now unknown.
{{ill|Yuge no Kiyoto|ja|弓削浄人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge no Kiyoto|qq=}}, Dōkyō's brother, hailed from {{ill|Wakae District, Osaka|ja|若江郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakae District, Osaka|qq=}}, but the Yuge clan appears to have had a broader distribution within Kawachi. In 764, figures such as Yuge Hirotari and Yuge no Okimaro resided in {{ill|Shibukawa District, Osaka|ja|渋川郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibukawa District, Osaka|qq=}}. Empress Kōken granted tax exemptions to {{ill|Ōgata District, Osaka|ja|大県郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgata District, Osaka|qq=}} and Wakae District, while halving taxes in {{ill|Asuka District, Osaka|ja|安宿郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka District, Osaka|qq=}} and Shiki District.
== Notes == {{reflist|2}}
== References == * {{Citation|太田亮|title=姓氏家系大辞典|publisher=Kadokawa Shoten|year=1963}} * {{Citation|佐伯有清|title=日本古代氏族事典|publisher=Yūzankaku|year=1994|author=中村英重}} * Takikawa, Masajirō, "Yuge no Dōkyō," in ''Jinbutsu Shin Nihonshi,'' Meiji Shoin, 1953 * Hōga, Toshio, ''Kozoku Keifu Shūsei,'' Ancient Clan Research Society, 1986 * Yokota, Ken'ichi, ''Dōkyō,'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, New Edition, 1988
== Historical Records == * ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'' * ''Nihon Shoki'' * ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' * ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku''

* '''Earthly Deities Lineage (Chigi系)''' : This lineage traced its ancestry to {{ill|Nekitsuma|ja|禰伎都麻|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nekitsuma|qq=}}, who was said to have been born from the water where {{ill|Ameoshihone no Mikoto|ja|天押穂根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameoshihone no Mikoto|qq=}} washed his hands.<ref name="s" />
== Relationship with Mononobe Clan ==

{{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Yuge Clan | image = | caption = | home_province = | titles = | founding_year = | dissolution_year = | notable_members = | related_clans = [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] }} '''Yuge Clan''' (弓削氏, ゆげうじ) was a [[clan]] that adopted "Yuge" as its name.
The Yuge clan led the [[Yuge no Be]] (弓削部), a group responsible for making bows in ancient Japan. The clan had multiple lineages, each with distinct ancestral traditions and regions of influence. The Yuge clan had close ties with the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]], with some lineages identifying as its cadet branches. Sub-branches included the {{ill|Hiraiwa Clan|ja|平岩氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiraiwa Clan|qq=}}.

The Yuge clan consisted of several lineages, making it difficult to categorize historical figures into specific branches. Based on the 9th-century ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'', the Yuge clan can be broadly classified into three lineages:
* '''Heavenly Lineage (Tenjin系)''' : This lineage claimed descent from {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高魂尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|qq=}} through {{ill|Amewakahiko|ja|天日鷲翔矢命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amewakahiko|qq=}}<ref name="s">''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'', Left Capital, Kami Lineage</ref> (also known as {{ill|Amebihashikakeyu no Mikoto|ja|天毘和志可気流夜命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amebihashikakeyu no Mikoto|qq=}}<ref>''Shinsen Shōjiroku'', Kawachi, Kami Lineage</ref>), serving as the principal administrators ({{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|lt=Tomonomiyatsuko|lt=Tomo no Miyatsuko|ja|伴造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomo no Miyatsuko|qq=}}) of the [[Yuge no Be]]. Their [[kabane]] was ''Muraji,'' later changed to ''Asomi'' during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}}.<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|13|12|2}}</ref>
* '''Descendants of the Heavenly Grandchild (Tenson系, Mononobe Lineage)''' : {{main|Yuge clan#Relationship with Mononobe clan}} : A branch of the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]].<ref>''Shinsen Shōjiroku'', Left Capital, Kami Lineage: "Yuge Sukune, same ancestor as Ishigami."</ref> [[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Mononobe no Moriya]] reportedly adopted the maternal surname Yuge O-muraji, and his descendants took the Yuge surname. Members of this lineage included [[:en:Dōkyō|Dōkyō]] and his brother {{ill|Yuge no Kiyoto|ja|弓削浄人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge no Kiyoto|qq=}}. With Dōkyō's favor under [[:en:Empress Kōken|Empress Kōken]] (later {{ill|Empress Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Shōtoku|qq=}}), the family gained significant prominence, with Yuge no Kiyoto achieving the Rank of [[:en:Dainagon|Dainagon]], and ten family members attaining Ranks of Fifth or higher. Previously bearing the kabane ''Muraji,'' Kiyoto was renamed ''Yuge Mikiyo Asomi,'' while others were renamed ''Yuge Asomi'' or ''Yuge Sukune.'' Following Dōkyō's downfall, their kabane reverted, and while some retained the title ''Yuge Sukune'' and held official posts, they failed to pass on their Ranks to subsequent generations.
* '''Earthly Deities Lineage (Chigi系)''' : This lineage traced its ancestry to {{ill|Nekitsuma|ja|禰伎都麻|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nekitsuma|qq=}}, who was said to have been born from the water where {{ill|Ameoshihone no Mikoto|ja|天押穂根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameoshihone no Mikoto|qq=}} washed his hands.<ref name="s" />
== Relationship with Mononobe Clan ==
The Yuge clan, based in Kawachi, shared a geographical and occupational connection with the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]]. Some scholars view them as separate groups,<ref>Yokota, Ken'ichi, ''Dōkyō,'' pp. 41–42.</ref> while others consider them a cadet branch.<ref>Takikawa, Masajirō, "Yuge no Dōkyō," in ''Jinbutsu Shin Nihonshi,'' pp. 193–194.</ref> The early Heian-period ''[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' states that [[:en:Mononobe no Okoshi|Mononobe no Oikoshi]] married Asa-hime and Kawaru-hime, women of the Yuge clan. Their child, Moriya, adopted the title Yuge O-muraji.<ref>''Sendai Kuji Hongi,'' Volume 5, ''Tenson Hongi.''</ref> The ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'' refers to Moriya as "Mononobe Yuge Moriya,"<ref>{{nihonshoki|Bidatsu|1|4|}}</ref> indicating his maternal lineage.<ref>Yokota, Ken'ichi, ''Dōkyō,'' p. 36.</ref>
A decree issued by Empress Kōken in [[:en:Tenpyō-hōji|Tenpyō-hōji]] (764) suggests that Dōkyō sought to succeed his ancestor's position as Minister. The "Minister" likely referred to Moriya, the last Mononobe O-muraji.<ref>Yokota, Ken'ichi, ''Dōkyō,'' p. 35.</ref>
The ''Nihon Shoki'' recounts that after Moriya's defeat, his children scattered, with half of his slaves and property allocated to [[:en:Shitennō-ji|Shitennō-ji]].<ref>{{nihonshoki|Suiko|1|1|}}</ref> The ''Shitennōji Ancient Records,'' written around the 10th century, describes descendants settling in five villages named Yuge. This supports the view that Moriya's descendants became part of the Yuge clan.<ref>Yokota, Ken'ichi, ''Dōkyō,'' pp. 39–40.</ref>
== Kibi Yuge Clan == The {{ill|Kibi Yuge no Be|ja|吉備弓削部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Yuge no Be|qq=}} included figures such as {{ill|Kibi Yuge no Kokū|ja|吉備弓削部虚空|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Yuge no Kokū|qq=}}. In [[:en:Bizen|Bizen Province]], {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|饒速日命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=}}, a supposed ancestor of the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]], was linked to the enshrinement of the {{ill|Totsuka-no-Tsurugi|ja|十握剣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Totsuka-no-Tsurugi|qq=}} at {{ill|Isonokami Futsunomitama Shrine|ja|石上布都魂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isonokami Futsunomitama Shrine|qq=}}. Nearby, various occupational groups formed, including one in modern [[:en:Kumenan|Kumenan]], Okayama Prefecture. ''[[:en:Hōnen|Hōnen]]'''s parents reportedly prayed at {{ill|Honzan-ji|ja|本山寺 (美咲町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honzan-ji|qq=}} in [[Misaki, Okayama]]. The temple was founded in 1110 by ''Yuge Shiko.'' Nearby is {{ill|Renkyū-ji|ja|蓮久寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Renkyū-ji|qq=}}, said to be the clan's ancestral temple.
== Distribution of Kawachi Yuge Clan == In modern [[:en:Yao, Osaka|Yao, Osaka]], the old course of the [[:en:Yamato River|Yamato River]] separates former {{ill|Shiki District, Osaka|ja|志紀郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki District, Osaka|qq=}} (Yuge) and {{ill|Wakae District, Osaka|ja|若江郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakae District, Osaka|qq=}} (Higashi Yuge). After the Edo-period river engineering, the stream is now {{ill|Nagase River|ja|長瀬川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagase River|qq=}}, though it was the Yamato River's main course in ancient times. Two {{ill|Yuge shrines|ja|弓削神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge shrines|qq=}} still exist on either side of the river. During the Nara period, {{ill|Yuge-ji|ja|弓削寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge-ji|qq=}}, which Empress Kōken visited, also stood here, but its location is now unknown.
{{ill|Yuge no Kiyoto|ja|弓削浄人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge no Kiyoto|qq=}}, Dōkyō's brother, hailed from {{ill|Wakae District, Osaka|ja|若江郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakae District, Osaka|qq=}}, but the Yuge clan appears to have had a broader distribution within Kawachi. In 764, figures such as Yuge Hirotari and Yuge no Okimaro resided in {{ill|Shibukawa District, Osaka|ja|渋川郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibukawa District, Osaka|qq=}}. Empress Kōken granted tax exemptions to {{ill|Ōgata District, Osaka|ja|大県郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgata District, Osaka|qq=}} and Wakae District, while halving taxes in {{ill|Asuka District, Osaka|ja|安宿郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka District, Osaka|qq=}} and Shiki District.

{{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Yuge Clan | image = | caption = | home_province = | titles = | founding_year = | dissolution_year = | notable_members = | related_clans = [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] }} '''Yuge Clan''' (弓削氏, ゆげうじ) was a [[clan]] that adopted "Yuge" as its name.
The Yuge clan led the [[Yuge no Be]] (弓削部), a group responsible for making bows in ancient Japan. The clan had multiple lineages, each with distinct ancestral traditions and regions of influence. The Yuge clan had close ties with the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]], with some lineages identifying as its cadet branches. Sub-branches included the {{ill|Hiraiwa Clan|ja|平岩氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiraiwa Clan|qq=}}.
== Lineages ==
The Yuge clan consisted of several lineages, making it difficult to categorize historical figures into specific branches. Based on the 9th-century ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'', the Yuge clan can be broadly classified into three lineages:
* '''Heavenly Lineage (Tenjin系)''' : This lineage claimed descent from {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高魂尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|qq=}} through {{ill|Amewakahiko|ja|天日鷲翔矢命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amewakahiko|qq=}}<ref name="s">''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'', Left Capital, Kami Lineage</ref> (also known as {{ill|Amebihashikakeyu no Mikoto|ja|天毘和志可気流夜命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amebihashikakeyu no Mikoto|qq=}}<ref>''Shinsen Shōjiroku'', Kawachi, Kami Lineage</ref>), serving as the principal administrators ({{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|lt=Tomonomiyatsuko|lt=Tomo no Miyatsuko|ja|伴造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomo no Miyatsuko|qq=}}) of the [[Yuge no Be]]. Their [[kabane]] was ''Muraji,'' later changed to ''Asomi'' during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}}.<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|13|12|2}}</ref>
* '''Descendants of the Heavenly Grandchild (Tenson系, Mononobe Lineage)''' : {{main|Yuge clan#Relationship with Mononobe clan}} : A branch of the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]].<ref>''Shinsen Shōjiroku'', Left Capital, Kami Lineage: "Yuge Sukune, same ancestor as Ishigami."</ref> [[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Mononobe no Moriya]] reportedly adopted the maternal surname Yuge O-muraji, and his descendants took the Yuge surname. Members of this lineage included [[:en:Dōkyō|Dōkyō]] and his brother {{ill|Yuge no Kiyoto|ja|弓削浄人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge no Kiyoto|qq=}}. With Dōkyō's favor under [[:en:Empress Kōken|Empress Kōken]] (later {{ill|Empress Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Shōtoku|qq=}}), the family gained significant prominence, with Yuge no Kiyoto achieving the Rank of [[:en:Dainagon|Dainagon]], and ten family members attaining Ranks of Fifth or higher. Previously bearing the kabane ''Muraji,'' Kiyoto was renamed ''Yuge Mikiyo Asomi,'' while others were renamed ''Yuge Asomi'' or ''Yuge Sukune.'' Following Dōkyō's downfall, their kabane reverted, and while some retained the title ''Yuge Sukune'' and held official posts, they failed to pass on their Ranks to subsequent generations.
* '''Earthly Deities Lineage (Chigi系)''' : This lineage traced its ancestry to {{ill|Nekitsuma|ja|禰伎都麻|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nekitsuma|qq=}}, who was said to have been born from the water where {{ill|Ameoshihone no Mikoto|ja|天押穂根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameoshihone no Mikoto|qq=}} washed his hands.<ref name="s" />
== Relationship with Mononobe Clan ==
The Yuge clan, based in Kawachi, shared a geographical and occupational connection with the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]]. Some scholars view them as separate groups,<ref>Yokota, Ken'ichi, ''Dōkyō,'' pp. 41–42.</ref> while others consider them a cadet branch.<ref>Takikawa, Masajirō, "Yuge no Dōkyō," in ''Jinbutsu Shin Nihonshi,'' pp. 193–194.</ref> The early Heian-period ''[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' states that [[:en:Mononobe no Okoshi|Mononobe no Oikoshi]] married Asa-hime and Kawaru-hime, women of the Yuge clan. Their child, Moriya, adopted the title Yuge O-muraji.<ref>''Sendai Kuji Hongi,'' Volume 5, ''Tenson Hongi.''</ref> The ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'' refers to Moriya as "Mononobe Yuge Moriya,"<ref>{{nihonshoki|Bidatsu|1|4|}}</ref> indicating his maternal lineage.<ref>Yokota, Ken'ichi, ''Dōkyō,'' p. 36.</ref>
A decree issued by Empress Kōken in [[:en:Tenpyō-hōji|Tenpyō-hōji]] (764) suggests that Dōkyō sought to succeed his ancestor's position as Minister. The "Minister" likely referred to Moriya, the last Mononobe O-muraji.<ref>Yokota, Ken'ichi, ''Dōkyō,'' p. 35.</ref>
The ''Nihon Shoki'' recounts that after Moriya's defeat, his children scattered, with half of his slaves and property allocated to [[:en:Shitennō-ji|Shitennō-ji]].<ref>{{nihonshoki|Suiko|1|1|}}</ref> The ''Shitennōji Ancient Records,'' written around the 10th century, describes descendants settling in five villages named Yuge. This supports the view that Moriya's descendants became part of the Yuge clan.<ref>Yokota, Ken'ichi, ''Dōkyō,'' pp. 39–40.</ref>
== Kibi Yuge Clan == The {{ill|Kibi Yuge no Be|ja|吉備弓削部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Yuge no Be|qq=}} included figures such as {{ill|Kibi Yuge no Kokū|ja|吉備弓削部虚空|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Yuge no Kokū|qq=}}. In [[:en:Bizen|Bizen Province]], {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|饒速日命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=}}, a supposed ancestor of the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]], was linked to the enshrinement of the {{ill|Totsuka-no-Tsurugi|ja|十握剣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Totsuka-no-Tsurugi|qq=}} at {{ill|Isonokami Futsunomitama Shrine|ja|石上布都魂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isonokami Futsunomitama Shrine|qq=}}. Nearby, various occupational groups formed, including one in modern [[:en:Kumenan|Kumenan]], Okayama Prefecture. ''[[:en:Hōnen|Hōnen]]'''s parents reportedly prayed at {{ill|Honzan-ji|ja|本山寺 (美咲町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honzan-ji|qq=}} in [[Misaki, Okayama]]. The temple was founded in 1110 by ''Yuge Shiko.'' Nearby is {{ill|Renkyū-ji|ja|蓮久寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Renkyū-ji|qq=}}, said to be the clan's ancestral temple.
== Distribution of Kawachi Yuge Clan == In modern [[:en:Yao, Osaka|Yao, Osaka]], the old course of the [[:en:Yamato River|Yamato River]] separates former {{ill|Shiki District, Osaka|ja|志紀郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki District, Osaka|qq=}} (Yuge) and {{ill|Wakae District, Osaka|ja|若江郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakae District, Osaka|qq=}} (Higashi Yuge). After the Edo-period river engineering, the stream is now {{ill|Nagase River|ja|長瀬川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagase River|qq=}}, though it was the Yamato River's main course in ancient times. Two {{ill|Yuge shrines|ja|弓削神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge shrines|qq=}} still exist on either side of the river. During the Nara period, {{ill|Yuge-ji|ja|弓削寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge-ji|qq=}}, which Empress Kōken visited, also stood here, but its location is now unknown.
{{ill|Yuge no Kiyoto|ja|弓削浄人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge no Kiyoto|qq=}}, Dōkyō's brother, hailed from {{ill|Wakae District, Osaka|ja|若江郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakae District, Osaka|qq=}}, but the Yuge clan appears to have had a broader distribution within Kawachi. In 764, figures such as Yuge Hirotari and Yuge no Okimaro resided in {{ill|Shibukawa District, Osaka|ja|渋川郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibukawa District, Osaka|qq=}}. Empress Kōken granted tax exemptions to {{ill|Ōgata District, Osaka|ja|大県郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgata District, Osaka|qq=}} and Wakae District, while halving taxes in {{ill|Asuka District, Osaka|ja|安宿郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka District, Osaka|qq=}} and Shiki District.

The Yuge clan, based in Kawachi, shared a geographical and occupational connection with the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]]. Some scholars view them as separate groups,<ref>Yokota, Ken'ichi, ''Dōkyō,'' pp. 41–42.</ref> while others consider them a cadet branch.<ref>Takikawa, Masajirō, "Yuge no Dōkyō," in ''Jinbutsu Shin Nihonshi,'' pp. 193–194.</ref> The early Heian-period ''[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' states that [[:en:Mononobe no Okoshi|Mononobe no Oikoshi]] married Asa-hime and Kawaru-hime, women of the Yuge clan. Their child, Moriya, adopted the title Yuge O-muraji.<ref>''Sendai Kuji Hongi,'' Volume 5, ''Tenson Hongi.''</ref> The ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'' refers to Moriya as "Mononobe Yuge Moriya,"<ref>{{nihonshoki|Bidatsu|1|4|}}</ref> indicating his maternal lineage.<ref>Yokota, Ken'ichi, ''Dōkyō,'' p. 36.</ref>
A decree issued by Empress Kōken in [[:en:Tenpyō-hōji|Tenpyō-hōji]] (764) suggests that Dōkyō sought to succeed his ancestor's position as Minister. The "Minister" likely referred to Moriya, the last Mononobe O-muraji.<ref>Yokota, Ken'ichi, ''Dōkyō,'' p. 35.</ref>
The ''Nihon Shoki'' recounts that after Moriya's defeat, his children scattered, with half of his slaves and property allocated to [[:en:Shitennō-ji|Shitennō-ji]].<ref>{{nihonshoki|Suiko|1|1|}}</ref> The ''Shitennōji Ancient Records,'' written around the 10th century, describes descendants settling in five villages named Yuge. This supports the view that Moriya's descendants became part of the Yuge clan.<ref>Yokota, Ken'ichi, ''Dōkyō,'' pp. 39–40.</ref>
== Kibi Yuge Clan == The {{ill|Kibi Yuge no Be|ja|吉備弓削部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Yuge no Be|qq=}} included figures such as {{ill|Kibi Yuge no Kokū|ja|吉備弓削部虚空|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Yuge no Kokū|qq=}}. In [[:en:Bizen|Bizen Province]], {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|饒速日命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=}}, a supposed ancestor of the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]], was linked to the enshrinement of the {{ill|Totsuka-no-Tsurugi|ja|十握剣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Totsuka-no-Tsurugi|qq=}} at {{ill|Isonokami Futsunomitama Shrine|ja|石上布都魂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isonokami Futsunomitama Shrine|qq=}}. Nearby, various occupational groups formed, including one in modern [[:en:Kumenan|Kumenan]], Okayama Prefecture. ''[[:en:Hōnen|Hōnen]]'''s parents reportedly prayed at {{ill|Honzan-ji|ja|本山寺 (美咲町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honzan-ji|qq=}} in [[Misaki, Okayama]]. The temple was founded in 1110 by ''Yuge Shiko.'' Nearby is {{ill|Renkyū-ji|ja|蓮久寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Renkyū-ji|qq=}}, said to be the clan's ancestral temple.
== Distribution of Kawachi Yuge Clan == In modern [[:en:Yao, Osaka|Yao, Osaka]], the old course of the [[:en:Yamato River|Yamato River]] separates former {{ill|Shiki District, Osaka|ja|志紀郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki District, Osaka|qq=}} (Yuge) and {{ill|Wakae District, Osaka|ja|若江郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakae District, Osaka|qq=}} (Higashi Yuge). After the Edo-period river engineering, the stream is now {{ill|Nagase River|ja|長瀬川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagase River|qq=}}, though it was the Yamato River's main course in ancient times. Two {{ill|Yuge shrines|ja|弓削神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge shrines|qq=}} still exist on either side of the river. During the Nara period, {{ill|Yuge-ji|ja|弓削寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge-ji|qq=}}, which Empress Kōken visited, also stood here, but its location is now unknown.
{{ill|Yuge no Kiyoto|ja|弓削浄人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge no Kiyoto|qq=}}, Dōkyō's brother, hailed from {{ill|Wakae District, Osaka|ja|若江郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakae District, Osaka|qq=}}, but the Yuge clan appears to have had a broader distribution within Kawachi. In 764, figures such as Yuge Hirotari and Yuge no Okimaro resided in {{ill|Shibukawa District, Osaka|ja|渋川郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibukawa District, Osaka|qq=}}. Empress Kōken granted tax exemptions to {{ill|Ōgata District, Osaka|ja|大県郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgata District, Osaka|qq=}} and Wakae District, while halving taxes in {{ill|Asuka District, Osaka|ja|安宿郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka District, Osaka|qq=}} and Shiki District.

[[sn:Yuge clan]] [[en:Yuge clan]] [[ja:弓削氏]] [[sn:uge clan]]
<!--Yuge clan--><!--Yuge clan--> [[Category:弓削氏]] [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:氏]] [[Category:Mononobe clan]]

{{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Yuge Clan | image = | caption = | home_province = | titles = | founding_year = | dissolution_year = | notable_members = | related_clans = [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] }} '''Yuge Clan''' (弓削氏, ゆげうじ) was a [[clan]] that adopted "Yuge" as its name.
The Yuge clan led the [[Yuge no Be]] (弓削部), a group responsible for making bows in ancient Japan. The clan had multiple lineages, each with distinct ancestral traditions and regions of influence. The Yuge clan had close ties with the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]], with some lineages identifying as its cadet branches. Sub-branches included the {{ill|Hiraiwa Clan|ja|平岩氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiraiwa Clan|qq=}}.
== Lineages ==
The Yuge clan consisted of several lineages, making it difficult to categorize historical figures into specific branches. Based on the 9th-century ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'', the Yuge clan can be broadly classified into three lineages:
* '''Heavenly Lineage (Tenjin系)''' : This lineage claimed descent from {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高魂尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|qq=}} through {{ill|Amewakahiko|ja|天日鷲翔矢命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amewakahiko|qq=}}<ref name="s">''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'', Left Capital, Kami Lineage</ref> (also known as {{ill|Amebihashikakeyu no Mikoto|ja|天毘和志可気流夜命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amebihashikakeyu no Mikoto|qq=}}<ref>''Shinsen Shōjiroku'', Kawachi, Kami Lineage</ref>), serving as the principal administrators ({{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|lt=Tomonomiyatsuko|lt=Tomo no Miyatsuko|ja|伴造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomo no Miyatsuko|qq=}}) of the [[Yuge no Be]]. Their [[kabane]] was ''Muraji,'' later changed to ''Asomi'' during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}}.<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|13|12|2}}</ref>
* '''Descendants of the Heavenly Grandchild (Tenson系, Mononobe Lineage)''' : {{main|Yuge clan#Relationship with Mononobe clan}} : A branch of the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]].<ref>''Shinsen Shōjiroku'', Left Capital, Kami Lineage: "Yuge Sukune, same ancestor as Ishigami."</ref> [[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Mononobe no Moriya]] reportedly adopted the maternal surname Yuge O-muraji, and his descendants took the Yuge surname. Members of this lineage included [[:en:Dōkyō|Dōkyō]] and his brother {{ill|Yuge no Kiyoto|ja|弓削浄人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge no Kiyoto|qq=}}. With Dōkyō's favor under [[:en:Empress Kōken|Empress Kōken]] (later {{ill|Empress Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Shōtoku|qq=}}), the family gained significant prominence, with Yuge no Kiyoto achieving the Rank of [[:en:Dainagon|Dainagon]], and ten family members attaining Ranks of Fifth or higher. Previously bearing the kabane ''Muraji,'' Kiyoto was renamed ''Yuge Mikiyo Asomi,'' while others were renamed ''Yuge Asomi'' or ''Yuge Sukune.'' Following Dōkyō's downfall, their kabane reverted, and while some retained the title ''Yuge Sukune'' and held official posts, they failed to pass on their Ranks to subsequent generations.
* '''Earthly Deities Lineage (Chigi系)''' : This lineage traced its ancestry to {{ill|Nekitsuma|ja|禰伎都麻|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nekitsuma|qq=}}, who was said to have been born from the water where {{ill|Ameoshihone no Mikoto|ja|天押穂根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameoshihone no Mikoto|qq=}} washed his hands.<ref name="s" />

Some scholars view them as separate groups,<ref>Yokota, Ken'ichi, ''Dōkyō,'' pp. 41–42.</ref> while others consider them a cadet branch.<ref>Takikawa, Masajirō, "Yuge no Dōkyō," in ''Jinbutsu Shin Nihonshi,'' pp. 193–194.</ref> The early Heian-period ''[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' states that [[:en:Mononobe no Okoshi|Mononobe no Oikoshi]] married Asa-hime and Kawaru-hime, women of the Yuge clan. Their child, Moriya, adopted the title Yuge O-muraji.<ref>''Sendai Kuji Hongi,'' Volume 5, ''Tenson Hongi.''</ref> The ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'' refers to Moriya as "Mononobe Yuge Moriya,"<ref>{{nihonshoki|Bidatsu|1|4|}}</ref> indicating his maternal lineage.<ref>Yokota, Ken'ichi, ''Dōkyō,'' p. 36.</ref>
A decree issued by Empress Kōken in [[:en:Tenpyō-hōji|Tenpyō-hōji]] (764) suggests that Dōkyō sought to succeed his ancestor's position as Minister. The "Minister" likely referred to Moriya, the last Mononobe O-muraji.<ref>Yokota, Ken'ichi, ''Dōkyō,'' p. 35.</ref>

This supports the view that Moriya's descendants became part of the Yuge clan.<ref>Yokota, Ken'ichi, ''Dōkyō,'' pp. 39–40.</ref>

The temple was founded in 1110 by ''Yuge Shiko.'' Nearby is {{ill|Renkyū-ji|ja|蓮久寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Renkyū-ji|qq=}}, said to be the clan's ancestral temple.
== Distribution of Kawachi Yuge Clan == In modern [[:en:Yao, Osaka|Yao, Osaka]], the old course of the [[:en:Yamato River|Yamato River]] separates former {{ill|Shiki District, Osaka|ja|志紀郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki District, Osaka|qq=}} (Yuge) and {{ill|Wakae District, Osaka|ja|若江郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakae District, Osaka|qq=}} (Higashi Yuge). Two {{ill|Yuge shrines|ja|弓削神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge shrines|qq=}} still exist on either side of the river. During the Nara period, {{ill|Yuge-ji|ja|弓削寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge-ji|qq=}}, which Empress Kōken visited, also stood here, but its location is now unknown.
{{ill|Yuge no Kiyoto|ja|弓削浄人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge no Kiyoto|qq=}}, Dōkyō's brother, hailed from {{ill|Wakae District, Osaka|ja|若江郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakae District, Osaka|qq=}}, but the Yuge clan appears to have had a broader distribution within Kawachi. In 764, figures such as Yuge Hirotari and Yuge no Okimaro resided in {{ill|Shibukawa District, Osaka|ja|渋川郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibukawa District, Osaka|qq=}}. The temple was founded in 1110 by ''Yuge Shiko.'' Nearby is {{ill|Renkyū-ji|ja|蓮久寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Renkyū-ji|qq=}}, said to be the clan's ancestral temple.
== Distribution of Kawachi Yuge Clan == In modern [[:en:Yao, Osaka|Yao, Osaka]], the old course of the [[:en:Yamato River|Yamato River]] separates former {{ill|Shiki District, Osaka|ja|志紀郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki District, Osaka|qq=}} (Yuge) and {{ill|Wakae District, Osaka|ja|若江郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakae District, Osaka|qq=}} (Higashi Yuge). After the Edo-period river engineering, the stream is now {{ill|Nagase River|ja|長瀬川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagase River|qq=}}, though it was the Yamato River's main course in ancient times. Two {{ill|Yuge shrines|ja|弓削神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge shrines|qq=}} still exist on either side of the river. During the Nara period, {{ill|Yuge-ji|ja|弓削寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge-ji|qq=}}, which Empress Kōken visited, also stood here, but its location is now unknown.
{{ill|Yuge no Kiyoto|ja|弓削浄人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge no Kiyoto|qq=}}, Dōkyō's brother, hailed from {{ill|Wakae District, Osaka|ja|若江郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakae District, Osaka|qq=}}, but the Yuge clan appears to have had a broader distribution within Kawachi. In 764, figures such as Yuge Hirotari and Yuge no Okimaro resided in {{ill|Shibukawa District, Osaka|ja|渋川郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibukawa District, Osaka|qq=}}. Empress Kōken granted tax exemptions to {{ill|Ōgata District, Osaka|ja|大県郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgata District, Osaka|qq=}} and Wakae District, while halving taxes in {{ill|Asuka District, Osaka|ja|安宿郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka District, Osaka|qq=}} and Shiki District.
== Notes == {{reflist|2}}
== References == * {{Citation|太田亮|title=姓氏家系大辞典|publisher=Kadokawa Shoten|year=1963}} * {{Citation|佐伯有清|title=日本古代氏族事典|publisher=Yūzankaku|year=1994|author=中村英重}} * Takikawa, Masajirō, "Yuge no Dōkyō," in ''Jinbutsu Shin Nihonshi,'' Meiji Shoin, 1953 * Hōga, Toshio, ''Kozoku Keifu Shūsei,'' Ancient Clan Research Society, 1986 * Yokota, Ken'ichi, ''Dōkyō,'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, New Edition, 1988

revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Yuge Clan | image = | caption = | home_province = | titles = | founding_year = | dissolution_year = | notable_members = | related_clans = [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] }} '''Yuge Clan''' (弓削氏, ゆげうじ) was a [[clan]] that adopted "Yuge" as its name.
The Yuge clan led the [[Yuge no Be]] (弓削部), a group responsible for making bows in ancient Japan. The clan had multiple lineages, each with distinct ancestral traditions and regions of influence. The Yuge clan had close ties with the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]], with some lineages identifying as its cadet branches. Sub-branches included the {{ill|Hiraiwa Clan|ja|平岩氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiraiwa Clan|qq=}}.
== Lineages ==
The Yuge clan consisted of several lineages, making it difficult to categorize historical figures into specific branches. Based on the 9th-century ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'', the Yuge clan can be broadly classified into three lineages:
* '''Heavenly Lineage (Tenjin系)''' : This lineage claimed descent from {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高魂尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|qq=}} through {{ill|Amewakahiko|ja|天日鷲翔矢命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amewakahiko|qq=}}<ref name="s">''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'', Left Capital, Kami Lineage</ref> (also known as {{ill|Amebihashikakeyu no Mikoto|ja|天毘和志可気流夜命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amebihashikakeyu no Mikoto|qq=}}<ref>''Shinsen Shōjiroku'', Kawachi, Kami Lineage</ref>), serving as the principal administrators ({{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|lt=Tomonomiyatsuko|lt=Tomo no Miyatsuko|ja|伴造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomo no Miyatsuko|qq=}}) of the [[Yuge no Be]]. Their [[kabane]] was ''Muraji,'' later changed to ''Asomi'' during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}}.<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|13|12|2}}</ref>
* '''Descendants of the Heavenly Grandchild (Tenson系, Mononobe Lineage)''' : {{main|Yuge clan#Relationship with Mononobe clan}} : A branch of the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]].<ref>''Shinsen Shōjiroku'', Left Capital, Kami Lineage: "Yuge Sukune, same ancestor as Ishigami."</ref> [[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Mononobe no Moriya]] reportedly adopted the maternal surname Yuge O-muraji, and his descendants took the Yuge surname. Members of this lineage included [[:en:Dōkyō|Dōkyō]] and his brother {{ill|Yuge no Kiyoto|ja|弓削浄人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge no Kiyoto|qq=}}. With Dōkyō's favor under [[:en:Empress Kōken|Empress Kōken]] (later {{ill|Empress Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Shōtoku|qq=}}), the family gained significant prominence, with Yuge no Kiyoto achieving the Rank of [[:en:Dainagon|Dainagon]], and ten family members attaining Ranks of Fifth or higher. Previously bearing the kabane ''Muraji,'' Kiyoto was renamed ''Yuge Mikiyo Asomi,'' while others were renamed ''Yuge Asomi'' or ''Yuge Sukune.'' Following Dōkyō's downfall, their kabane reverted, and while some retained the title ''Yuge Sukune'' and held official posts, they failed to pass on their Ranks to subsequent generations.
* '''Earthly Deities Lineage (Chigi系)''' : This lineage traced its ancestry to {{ill|Nekitsuma|ja|禰伎都麻|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nekitsuma|qq=}}, who was said to have been born from the water where {{ill|Ameoshihone no Mikoto|ja|天押穂根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameoshihone no Mikoto|qq=}} washed his hands.<ref name="s" />
== Relationship with Mononobe Clan ==
The Yuge clan, based in Kawachi, shared a geographical and occupational connection with the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]]. Some scholars view them as separate groups,<ref>Yokota, Ken'ichi, ''Dōkyō,'' pp. 41–42.</ref> while others consider them a cadet branch.<ref>Takikawa, Masajirō, "Yuge no Dōkyō," in ''Jinbutsu Shin Nihonshi,'' pp. 193–194.</ref> The early Heian-period ''[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' states that [[:en:Mononobe no Okoshi|Mononobe no Oikoshi]] married Asa-hime and Kawaru-hime, women of the Yuge clan. Their child, Moriya, adopted the title Yuge O-muraji.<ref>''Sendai Kuji Hongi,'' Volume 5, ''Tenson Hongi.''</ref> The ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'' refers to Moriya as "Mononobe Yuge Moriya,"<ref>{{nihonshoki|Bidatsu|1|4|}}</ref> indicating his maternal lineage.<ref>Yokota, Ken'ichi, ''Dōkyō,'' p. 36.</ref>
A decree issued by Empress Kōken in [[:en:Tenpyō-hōji|Tenpyō-hōji]] (764) suggests that Dōkyō sought to succeed his ancestor's position as Minister. The "Minister" likely referred to Moriya, the last Mononobe O-muraji.<ref>Yokota, Ken'ichi, ''Dōkyō,'' p. 35.</ref>
The ''Nihon Shoki'' recounts that after Moriya's defeat, his children scattered, with half of his slaves and property allocated to [[:en:Shitennō-ji|Shitennō-ji]].<ref>{{nihonshoki|Suiko|1|1|}}</ref> The ''Shitennōji Ancient Records,'' written around the 10th century, describes descendants settling in five villages named Yuge. This supports the view that Moriya's descendants became part of the Yuge clan.<ref>Yokota, Ken'ichi, ''Dōkyō,'' pp. 39–40.</ref>
== Kibi Yuge Clan == The {{ill|Kibi Yuge no Be|ja|吉備弓削部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Yuge no Be|qq=}} included figures such as {{ill|Kibi Yuge no Kokū|ja|吉備弓削部虚空|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Yuge no Kokū|qq=}}. In [[:en:Bizen|Bizen Province]], {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|饒速日命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=}}, a supposed ancestor of the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]], was linked to the enshrinement of the {{ill|Totsuka-no-Tsurugi|ja|十握剣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Totsuka-no-Tsurugi|qq=}} at {{ill|Isonokami Futsunomitama Shrine|ja|石上布都魂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isonokami Futsunomitama Shrine|qq=}}. Nearby, various occupational groups formed, including one in modern [[:en:Kumenan|Kumenan]], Okayama Prefecture. ''[[:en:Hōnen|Hōnen]]'''s parents reportedly prayed at {{ill|Honzan-ji|ja|本山寺 (美咲町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honzan-ji|qq=}} in [[Misaki, Okayama]]. The temple was founded in 1110 by ''Yuge Shiko.'' Nearby is {{ill|Renkyū-ji|ja|蓮久寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Renkyū-ji|qq=}}, said to be the clan's ancestral temple.
== Distribution of Kawachi Yuge Clan == In modern [[:en:Yao, Osaka|Yao, Osaka]], the old course of the [[:en:Yamato River|Yamato River]] separates former {{ill|Shiki District, Osaka|ja|志紀郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki District, Osaka|qq=}} (Yuge) and {{ill|Wakae District, Osaka|ja|若江郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakae District, Osaka|qq=}} (Higashi Yuge). After the Edo-period river engineering, the stream is now {{ill|Nagase River|ja|長瀬川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagase River|qq=}}, though it was the Yamato River's main course in ancient times. Two {{ill|Yuge shrines|ja|弓削神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge shrines|qq=}} still exist on either side of the river. During the Nara period, {{ill|Yuge-ji|ja|弓削寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge-ji|qq=}}, which Empress Kōken visited, also stood here, but its location is now unknown.
{{ill|Yuge no Kiyoto|ja|弓削浄人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge no Kiyoto|qq=}}, Dōkyō's brother, hailed from {{ill|Wakae District, Osaka|ja|若江郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakae District, Osaka|qq=}}, but the Yuge clan appears to have had a broader distribution within Kawachi. In 764, figures such as Yuge Hirotari and Yuge no Okimaro resided in {{ill|Shibukawa District, Osaka|ja|渋川郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibukawa District, Osaka|qq=}}. Empress Kōken granted tax exemptions to {{ill|Ōgata District, Osaka|ja|大県郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgata District, Osaka|qq=}} and Wakae District, while halving taxes in {{ill|Asuka District, Osaka|ja|安宿郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka District, Osaka|qq=}} and Shiki District.
== Notes == {{reflist|2}}
== References == * {{Citation|太田亮|title=姓氏家系大辞典|publisher=Kadokawa Shoten|year=1963}} * {{Citation|佐伯有清|title=日本古代氏族事典|publisher=Yūzankaku|year=1994|author=中村英重}} * Takikawa, Masajirō, "Yuge no Dōkyō," in ''Jinbutsu Shin Nihonshi,'' Meiji Shoin, 1953 * Hōga, Toshio, ''Kozoku Keifu Shūsei,'' Ancient Clan Research Society, 1986 * Yokota, Ken'ichi, ''Dōkyō,'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, New Edition, 1988
== Historical Records == * ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'' * ''Nihon Shoki'' * ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' * ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku''

== Kibi Yuge Clan == The {{ill|Kibi Yuge no Be|ja|吉備弓削部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Yuge no Be|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} included figures such as {{ill|Kibi Yuge no Kokū|ja|吉備弓削部虚空|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Yuge no Kokū|qq=}}. revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Yuge Clan | image = | caption = | home_province = | titles = | founding_year = | dissolution_year = | notable_members = | related_clans = [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] }} '''Yuge Clan''' (弓削氏, ゆげうじ) was a [[:en:Clan|Clan]] that adopted "Yuge" as its name.
The Yuge clan led the [[Yuge no Be]] (弓削部), a group responsible for making bows in ancient Japan. The clan had multiple lineages, each with distinct ancestral traditions and regions of influence. The Yuge clan had close ties with the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]], with some lineages identifying as its cadet branches. Sub-branches included the {{ill|Hiraiwa Clan|ja|平岩氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiraiwa Clan|qq=}}.
== Lineages ==
The Yuge clan consisted of several lineages, making it difficult to categorize historical figures into specific branches. Based on the 9th-century ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'', the Yuge clan can be broadly classified into three lineages:
* '''Heavenly Lineage (Tenjin系)''' : This lineage claimed descent from {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高魂尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}} through {{ill|Amewakahiko|ja|天日鷲翔矢命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amewakahiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天日鷲神}}<ref name="s">''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'', Left Capital, Kami Lineage</ref> (also known as {{ill|Amebihashikakeyu no Mikoto|ja|天毘和志可気流夜命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amebihashikakeyu no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref>''Shinsen Shōjiroku'', Kawachi, Kami Lineage</ref>), serving as the principal administrators ({{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|lt=Tomonomiyatsuko|lt=Tomo no Miyatsuko|ja|伴造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomo no Miyatsuko|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) of the [[Yuge no Be]]. Their [[kabane]] was ''Muraji,'' later changed to ''Asomi'' during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|13|12|2}}</ref>
* '''Descendants of the Heavenly Grandchild (Tenson系, Mononobe Lineage)''' : {{main|Yuge clan#Relationship with Mononobe clan}} : A branch of the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]].<ref>''Shinsen Shōjiroku'', Left Capital, Kami Lineage: "Yuge Sukune, same ancestor as Ishigami."</ref> [[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Mononobe no Moriya]] reportedly adopted the maternal surname Yuge O-muraji, and his descendants took the Yuge surname. Members of this lineage included [[:en:Dōkyō|Dōkyō]] and his brother {{ill|Yuge no Kiyoto|ja|弓削浄人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge no Kiyoto|qq=}}. With Dōkyō's favor under [[:en:Empress Kōken|Empress Kōken]] (later {{ill|Empress Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Shōtoku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇}}), the family gained significant prominence, with Yuge no Kiyoto achieving the Rank of [[:en:Dainagon|Dainagon]], and ten family members attaining Ranks of Fifth or higher. Previously bearing the kabane ''Muraji,'' Kiyoto was renamed ''Yuge Mikiyo Asomi,'' while others were renamed ''Yuge Asomi'' or ''Yuge Sukune.'' Following Dōkyō's downfall, their kabane reverted, and while some retained the title ''Yuge Sukune'' and held official posts, they failed to pass on their Ranks to subsequent generations.
* '''Earthly Deities Lineage (Chigi系)''' : This lineage traced its ancestry to {{ill|Nekitsuma|ja|禰伎都麻|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nekitsuma|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who was said to have been born from the water where {{ill|Ameoshihone no Mikoto|ja|天押穂根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameoshihone no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} washed his hands.<ref name="s" />
== Relationship with Mononobe Clan ==
The Yuge clan, based in Kawachi, shared a geographical and occupational connection with the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]]. Some scholars view them as separate groups,<ref>Yokota, Ken'ichi, ''Dōkyō,'' pp. 41–42.</ref> while others consider them a cadet branch.<ref>Takikawa, Masajirō, "Yuge no Dōkyō," in ''Jinbutsu Shin Nihonshi,'' pp. 193–194.</ref> The early Heian-period ''[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' states that [[:en:Mononobe no Okoshi|Mononobe no Oikoshi]] married Asa-hime and Kawaru-hime, women of the Yuge clan. Their child, Moriya, adopted the title Yuge O-muraji.<ref>''Sendai Kuji Hongi,'' Volume 5, ''Tenson Hongi.''</ref> The ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'' refers to Moriya as "Mononobe Yuge Moriya,"<ref>{{nihonshoki|Bidatsu|1|4|}}</ref> indicating his maternal lineage.<ref>Yokota, Ken'ichi, ''Dōkyō,'' p. 36.</ref>
A decree issued by Empress Kōken in [[:en:Tenpyō-hōji|Tenpyō-hōji]] (764) suggests that Dōkyō sought to succeed his ancestor's position as Minister. The "Minister" likely referred to Moriya, the last Mononobe O-muraji.<ref>Yokota, Ken'ichi, ''Dōkyō,'' p. 35.</ref>
The ''Nihon Shoki'' recounts that after Moriya's defeat, his children scattered, with half of his slaves and property allocated to [[:en:Shitennō-ji|Shitennō-ji]].<ref>{{nihonshoki|Suiko|1|1|}}</ref> The ''Shitennōji Ancient Records,'' written around the 10th century, describes descendants settling in five villages named Yuge. This supports the view that Moriya's descendants became part of the Yuge clan.<ref>Yokota, Ken'ichi, ''Dōkyō,'' pp. 39–40.</ref>
== Kibi Yuge Clan == The {{ill|Kibi Yuge no Be|ja|吉備弓削部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Yuge no Be|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} included figures such as {{ill|Kibi Yuge no Kokū|ja|吉備弓削部虚空|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Yuge no Kokū|qq=}}. In [[:en:Bizen|Bizen Province]], {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|饒速日命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}}, a supposed ancestor of the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]], was linked to the enshrinement of the {{ill|Totsuka-no-Tsurugi|ja|十握剣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Totsuka-no-Tsurugi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 十束剣}} at {{ill|Isonokami Futsunomitama Shrine|ja|石上布都魂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isonokami Futsunomitama Shrine|qq=}}. Nearby, various occupational groups formed, including one in modern [[:en:Kumenan|Kumenan]], Okayama Prefecture. ''[[:en:Hōnen|Hōnen]]'''s parents reportedly prayed at {{ill|Honzan-ji|ja|本山寺 (美咲町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honzan-ji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[Misaki, Okayama]]. The temple was founded in 1110 by ''Yuge Shiko.'' Nearby is {{ill|Renkyū-ji|ja|蓮久寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Renkyū-ji|qq=}}, said to be the clan's ancestral temple.
== Distribution of Kawachi Yuge Clan == In modern [[:en:Yao, Osaka|Yao, Osaka]], the old course of the [[:en:Yamato River|Yamato River]] separates former {{ill|Shiki District, Osaka|ja|志紀郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki District, Osaka|qq=}} (Yuge) and {{ill|Wakae District, Osaka|ja|若江郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakae District, Osaka|qq=}} (Higashi Yuge). After the Edo-period river engineering, the stream is now {{ill|Nagase River|ja|長瀬川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagase River|qq=}}, though it was the Yamato River's main course in ancient times. Two {{ill|Yuge shrines|ja|弓削神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge shrines|qq=}} still exist on either side of the river. During the Nara period, {{ill|Yuge-ji|ja|弓削寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge-ji|qq=}}, which Empress Kōken visited, also stood here, but its location is now unknown.
{{ill|Yuge no Kiyoto|ja|弓削浄人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge no Kiyoto|qq=}}, Dōkyō's brother, hailed from {{ill|Wakae District, Osaka|ja|若江郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakae District, Osaka|qq=}}, but the Yuge clan appears to have had a broader distribution within Kawachi. In 764, figures such as Yuge Hirotari and Yuge no Okimaro resided in {{ill|Shibukawa District, Osaka|ja|渋川郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibukawa District, Osaka|qq=}}. Empress Kōken granted tax exemptions to {{ill|Ōgata District, Osaka|ja|大県郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgata District, Osaka|qq=}} and Wakae District, while halving taxes in {{ill|Asuka District, Osaka|ja|安宿郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka District, Osaka|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 安宿部郡}} and Shiki District.
== Notes == {{reflist|2}}
== References == * {{Citation|太田亮|title=姓氏家系大辞典|publisher=Kadokawa Shoten|year=1963}} * {{Citation|佐伯有清|title=日本古代氏族事典|publisher=Yūzankaku|year=1994|author=中村英重}} * Takikawa, Masajirō, "Yuge no Dōkyō," in ''Jinbutsu Shin Nihonshi,'' Meiji Shoin, 1953 * Hōga, Toshio, ''Kozoku Keifu Shūsei,'' Ancient Clan Research Society, 1986 * Yokota, Ken'ichi, ''Dōkyō,'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, New Edition, 1988
== Historical Records == * ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'' * ''Nihon Shoki'' * ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' * ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku''

{{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Yuge Clan | image = | caption = | home_province = | titles = | founding_year = | dissolution_year = | notable_members = | related_clans = [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] }} '''Yuge Clan''' (弓削氏, ゆげうじ) was a [[:en:Clan|Clan]] that adopted "Yuge" as its name.
The Yuge clan led the [[Yuge no Be]] (弓削部), a group responsible for making bows in ancient Japan. The clan had multiple lineages, each with distinct ancestral traditions and regions of influence. The Yuge clan had close ties with the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]], with some lineages identifying as its cadet branches. Sub-branches included the {{ill|Hiraiwa Clan|ja|平岩氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiraiwa Clan|qq=}}.
== Lineages ==
The Yuge clan consisted of several lineages, making it difficult to categorize historical figures into specific branches. Based on the 9th-century ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'', the Yuge clan can be broadly classified into three lineages:
* '''Heavenly Lineage (Tenjin系)''' : This lineage claimed descent from {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高魂尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}} through {{ill|Amewakahiko|ja|天日鷲翔矢命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amewakahiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天日鷲神}}<ref name="s">''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'', Left Capital, Kami Lineage</ref> (also known as {{ill|Amebihashikakeyu no Mikoto|ja|天毘和志可気流夜命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amebihashikakeyu no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref>''Shinsen Shōjiroku'', Kawachi, Kami Lineage</ref>), serving as the principal administrators ({{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|lt=Tomonomiyatsuko|lt=Tomo no Miyatsuko|ja|伴造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomo no Miyatsuko|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) of the [[Yuge no Be]]. Their [[kabane]] was ''Muraji,'' later changed to ''Asomi'' during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>{{nihonshoki|Tenmu|13|12|2}}</ref>
* '''Descendants of the Heavenly Grandchild (Tenson系, Mononobe Lineage)''' : {{main|Yuge clan#Relationship with Mononobe clan}} : A branch of the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]].<ref>''Shinsen Shōjiroku'', Left Capital, Kami Lineage: "Yuge Sukune, same ancestor as Ishigami."</ref> [[:en:Mononobe no Moriya|Mononobe no Moriya]] reportedly adopted the maternal surname Yuge O-muraji, and his descendants took the Yuge surname. Members of this lineage included [[:en:Dōkyō|Dōkyō]] and his brother {{ill|Yuge no Kiyoto|ja|弓削浄人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge no Kiyoto|qq=}}. With Dōkyō's favor under [[:en:Empress Kōken|Empress Kōken]] (later {{ill|Empress Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Shōtoku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇}}), the family gained significant prominence, with Yuge no Kiyoto achieving the Rank of [[:en:Dainagon|Dainagon]], and ten family members attaining Ranks of Fifth or higher. Previously bearing the kabane ''Muraji,'' Kiyoto was renamed ''Yuge Mikiyo Asomi,'' while others were renamed ''Yuge Asomi'' or ''Yuge Sukune.'' Following Dōkyō's downfall, their kabane reverted, and while some retained the title ''Yuge Sukune'' and held official posts, they failed to pass on their Ranks to subsequent generations.
* '''Earthly Deities Lineage (Chigi系)''' : This lineage traced its ancestry to {{ill|Nekitsuma|ja|禰伎都麻|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nekitsuma|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who was said to have been born from the water where {{ill|Ameoshihone no Mikoto|ja|天押穂根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameoshihone no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} washed his hands.<ref name="s" />
== Relationship with Mononobe Clan ==
The Yuge clan, based in Kawachi, shared a geographical and occupational connection with the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]]. Some scholars view them as separate groups,<ref>Yokota, Ken'ichi, ''Dōkyō,'' pp. 41–42.</ref> while others consider them a cadet branch.<ref>Takikawa, Masajirō, "Yuge no Dōkyō," in ''Jinbutsu Shin Nihonshi,'' pp. 193–194.</ref> The early Heian-period ''[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' states that [[:en:Mononobe no Okoshi|Mononobe no Oikoshi]] married Asa-hime and Kawaru-hime, women of the Yuge clan. Their child, Moriya, adopted the title Yuge O-muraji.<ref>''Sendai Kuji Hongi,'' Volume 5, ''Tenson Hongi.''</ref> The ''[[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]]'' refers to Moriya as "Mononobe Yuge Moriya,"<ref>{{nihonshoki|Bidatsu|1|4|}}</ref> indicating his maternal lineage.<ref>Yokota, Ken'ichi, ''Dōkyō,'' p. 36.</ref>
A decree issued by Empress Kōken in [[:en:Tenpyō-hōji|Tenpyō-hōji]] (764) suggests that Dōkyō sought to succeed his ancestor's position as Minister. The "Minister" likely referred to Moriya, the last Mononobe O-muraji.<ref>Yokota, Ken'ichi, ''Dōkyō,'' p. 35.</ref>
The ''Nihon Shoki'' recounts that after Moriya's defeat, his children scattered, with half of his slaves and property allocated to [[:en:Shitennō-ji|Shitennō-ji]].<ref>{{nihonshoki|Suiko|1|1|}}</ref> The ''Shitennōji Ancient Records,'' written around the 10th century, describes descendants settling in five villages named Yuge. This supports the view that Moriya's descendants became part of the Yuge clan.<ref>Yokota, Ken'ichi, ''Dōkyō,'' pp. 39–40.</ref>
== Kibi Yuge Clan == The {{ill|Kibi Yuge no Be|ja|吉備弓削部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Yuge no Be|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} included figures such as {{ill|Kibi Yuge no Kokū|ja|吉備弓削部虚空|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Yuge no Kokū|qq=}}. In [[:en:Bizen|Bizen Province]], {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|饒速日命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}}, a supposed ancestor of the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]], was linked to the enshrinement of the {{ill|Totsuka-no-Tsurugi|ja|十握剣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Totsuka-no-Tsurugi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 十束剣}} at {{ill|Isonokami Futsunomitama Shrine|ja|石上布都魂神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isonokami Futsunomitama Shrine|qq=}}. Nearby, various occupational groups formed, including one in modern [[:en:Kumenan|Kumenan]], Okayama Prefecture. ''[[:en:Hōnen|Hōnen]]'''s parents reportedly prayed at {{ill|Honzan-ji|ja|本山寺 (美咲町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Honzan-ji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Misaki, Okayama|Misaki, Okayama]]. The temple was founded in 1110 by ''Yuge Shiko.'' Nearby is {{ill|Renkyū-ji|ja|蓮久寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Renkyū-ji|qq=}}, said to be the clan's ancestral temple.
== Distribution of Kawachi Yuge Clan == In modern [[:en:Yao, Osaka|Yao, Osaka]], the old course of the [[:en:Yamato River|Yamato River]] separates former {{ill|Shiki District, Osaka|ja|志紀郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiki District, Osaka|qq=}} (Yuge) and {{ill|Wakae District, Osaka|ja|若江郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakae District, Osaka|qq=}} (Higashi Yuge). After the Edo-period river engineering, the stream is now {{ill|Nagase River|ja|長瀬川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagase River|qq=}}, though it was the Yamato River's main course in ancient times. Two {{ill|Yuge shrines|ja|弓削神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge shrines|qq=}} still exist on either side of the river. During the Nara period, {{ill|Yuge-ji|ja|弓削寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge-ji|qq=}}, which Empress Kōken visited, also stood here, but its location is now unknown.
{{ill|Yuge no Kiyoto|ja|弓削浄人|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuge no Kiyoto|qq=}}, Dōkyō's brother, hailed from {{ill|Wakae District, Osaka|ja|若江郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakae District, Osaka|qq=}}, but the Yuge clan appears to have had a broader distribution within Kawachi. In 764, figures such as Yuge Hirotari and Yuge no Okimaro resided in {{ill|Shibukawa District, Osaka|ja|渋川郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibukawa District, Osaka|qq=}}. Empress Kōken granted tax exemptions to {{ill|Ōgata District, Osaka|ja|大県郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōgata District, Osaka|qq=}} and Wakae District, while halving taxes in {{ill|Asuka District, Osaka|ja|安宿郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka District, Osaka|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 安宿部郡}} and Shiki District.
== Notes == {{reflist|2}}
== References == * {{Citation|太田亮|title=姓氏家系大辞典|publisher=Kadokawa Shoten|year=1963}} * {{Citation|佐伯有清|title=日本古代氏族事典|publisher=Yūzankaku|year=1994|author=中村英重}} * Takikawa, Masajirō, "Yuge no Dōkyō," in ''Jinbutsu Shin Nihonshi,'' Meiji Shoin, 1953 * Hōga, Toshio, ''Kozoku Keifu Shūsei,'' Ancient Clan Research Society, 1986 * Yokota, Ken'ichi, ''Dōkyō,'' Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, New Edition, 1988
== Historical Records == * ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'' * ''Nihon Shoki'' * ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' * ''Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku''

{{Infobox Japanese clan | name = Yuge Clan | image = | caption = | home_province = | titles = | founding_year = | dissolution_year = | notable_members = | related_clans = [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] }} '''Yuge Clan''' (弓削氏, ゆげうじ) was a [[:en:Clan|Clan]] that adopted "Yuge" as its name.
The Yuge clan led the [[Yuge no Be]] (弓削部), a group responsible for making bows in ancient Japan. The clan had multiple lineages, each with distinct ancestral traditions and regions of influence. The Yuge clan had close ties with the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]], with some lineages identifying as its cadet branches. Sub-branches included the {{ill|Hiraiwa Clan|ja|平岩氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiraiwa Clan|qq=}}.

With Dōkyō's favor under [[:en:Empress Kōken|Empress Kōken]] (later {{ill|Empress Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Shōtoku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇}}), the family gained significant prominence, with Yuge no Kiyoto achieving the Rank of [[:en:Dainagon|Dainagon]], and ten family members attaining Ranks of Fifth or higher. Following Dōkyō's downfall, their kabane reverted, and while some retained the title ''Yuge Sukune'' and held official posts, they failed to pass on their Ranks to subsequent generations.
* '''Earthly Deities Lineage (Chigi系)''' : This lineage traced its ancestry to {{ill|Nekitsuma|ja|禰伎都麻|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nekitsuma|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who was said to have been born from the water where {{ill|Ameoshihone no Mikoto|ja|天押穂根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameoshihone no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} washed his hands.<ref name="s" />
